arguments.task: Research Bright Data using official first-party pages only. Open and inspect Bright Data's developer documentation, authentication docs, API docs, pricing or access pages, support docs, GitHub pages, and any MCP-related official pages. Extract: one-line product description; available auth methods for public APIs (only if explicitly documented); whether access is self-serve free/trial/paid/admin approval/partner-gated; API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None) and breadth; whether an MCP server exists and notes; buildability verdict for building an AI-agent toolkit today; any rate limit notes; notable signals; and exact supporting URLs. Do not use third-party blogs unless needed to locate first-party pages. Return findings with source URLs and quote snippets where possible.

input.thought: Use the browser tool to inspect official Bright Data pages and collect verifiable evidence.

raw_result.description: Web data platform offering proxies, pre-built scrapers, datasets, and AI-ready web data APIs.
raw_result.auth_notes: Public docs indicate API token/Bearer authentication for API access and username/password credentials for proxy services; evidence gathered points to both, but endpoint-by-endpoint auth coverage was not fully verified from all product docs.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public pricing page shows self-serve signup with a free trial and free-tier offers for some products, including MCP-related usage.

api_surface.breadth: Documented API reference covers multiple REST products including Web Unlocker, SERP/API-style scraping flows, datasets, and proxy/account-related capabilities; no GraphQL evidence was found in gathered sources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Bright Data MCP exists ('Web MCP'), with Bright Data docs and an official GitHub repository; gathered evidence indicates remote/cloud-hosted and local/self-hosted options plus a free monthly allowance.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered evidence indicates rate/concurrency limits vary by plan/zone and exceeding limits can result in HTTP 429; exact numeric limits were not verified from a first-party rate-limit table in the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Bright Data has dedicated AI-agent/MCP positioning, an official MCP server, free monthly MCP allowance, and broad scraping/unlocking tooling oriented toward real-time web extraction.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some specifics remain ambiguous from the already gathered evidence: exact normalized auth method mapping across all Bright Data products, precise access-tier classification between free vs trial, and exact first-party numeric rate limits.

result.description: Web data platform offering proxies, pre-built scrapers, datasets, and AI-ready web data APIs.
